Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

content.thinking:
The user is investigating why switching back to claude agent after switching to a different agent still uses Opus 4.6 instead of the configured Fable 5.

Looking at the code, I can see the issue:

In `sessionManager.ts` lines 540-558, when creating an ACP provider:
```typescript
return new AcpProvider({
  command: backend?.command ?? config.agent.command,
  args: backend?.args ?? config.agent.args,
  cwd: config.sessionCwd,
  env,
  promptRetries: config.acpPromptRetries,
  promptRetryBaseMs: config.acpPromptRetryBaseMs,
  acpModel: backend ? (backend.model ?? "") : config.acpModel,
  acpEffort: backend ? (backend.effort ?? "") : config.acpEffort,
  sessionCwd: config.sessionCwd,
  resumeSessionId,
});
```

The critical line is:
```typescript
acpModel: backend ? (backend.model ?? "") : config.acpModel,
```

This logic says:
- If `backend` exists (i.e., user is using an agent defined in acp-providers.json), use `backend.model` if it exists, otherwise use empty string `""`
- If `backend` doesn't exist (i.e., using default .env agent), use `config.acpModel`

The problem is: when switching agents with `/agent`, if the acp-providers.json entry doesn't have a `model` field, it uses empty string `""` instead of falling back to `.env`'s `ACP_MODEL`. An empty string means "don't pin the model", which allows the agent to use whatever model it was previously using or its own default.

This is a bug in the fallback logic. The code comment even says:
"including the model/effort pin (undefined = leave the adapter's own default untouched, NOT fall back to .env's pin)."

The issue is that when an acp-providers.json entry lacks a model field, it intentionally leaves the adapter's default untouched rather than applying the .env pin. So if the user switches back to "claude" expecting Fable 5 from their .env configuration, but the acp-providers.json entry doesn't specify a model, the pin won't be applied and the previous model persists.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Agent switching model pin fallback issue discovered</title>
  <subtitle>Switching agents without explicit model pin in acp-providers.json bypasses .env ACP_MODEL configuration</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts createProvider() uses `backend ? (backend.model ?? "") : config.acpModel` for acpModel parameter</fact>
    <fact>When backend exists but backend.model is undefined, empty string "" is passed instead of config.acpModel from .env</fact>
    <fact>Empty string acpModel means no model pin applied, allowing agent to use previous or default model</fact>
    <fact>User switched agents using /agent command expecting .env ACP_MODEL=claude-fable-5 to apply</fact>
    <fact>acp-providers.json entry for "claude" backend lacks explicit model field, resulting in no model pin</fact>
    <fact>Previous session had manually set model to opus-4-6 via /model command, which persisted through agent switch</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation revealed the root cause of model persistence across agent switches. When a user switches agents using /agent, the sessionManager's createProvider() method applies model pinning based on the backend definition from acp-providers.json. The current logic has a critical fallback issue: when a backend is defined but lacks a model field, it uses an empty string "" rather than falling back to the .env file's ACP_MODEL configuration. An empty string means "no pin," which leaves the model selection to the agent's own defaults or previous state. In this case, the user had previously used /model opus to switch to Opus 4.6, then switched agents expecting the .env's ACP_MODEL=claude-fable-5 to take effect, but because the acp-providers.json "claude" entry had no model field, no pin was applied, and the agent continued using Opus 4.6. This reveals a gap between user expectations (agent switch should respect .env model config) and actual behavior (agent switch only respects per-backend model pins, .env is only used when no backend is defined).</narrative>

content.thinking:
This is the KEY finding! Looking at the Claude Code settings:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json",
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [...]
  },
  "model": "opus[1m]",  // <-- HERE IS THE PROBLEM!
  ...
}
```

The Claude Code settings.json has `"model": "opus[1m]"` set at the agent level. This is a global setting that applies to ALL sessions started by this Claude Code agent, regardless of what the bridge tries to configure via ACP session/set_config_option.

So the flow is:
1. User switches agents using /agent claude
2. Bridge correctly reads acp-providers.json which has "model": "claude-fable-5"
3. Bridge passes this to AcpProvider constructor
4. Bridge spawns claude-agent-acp subprocess
5. claude-agent-acp reads its own ~/.claude/settings.json which has "model": "opus[1m]"
6. This settings.json model takes precedence over the session/set_config_option call from the bridge
7. Result: session runs on Opus 4.6 despite correct bridge configuration

This is a critical discovery about the interaction between bridge-level configuration and agent-level configuration. The user must have set `"model": "opus[1m]"` in their Claude Code settings previously (probably when they were testing /model or experimenting), and that global setting is now overriding the bridge's per-session model pins.

The fix is to remove the "model" line from ~/.claude/settings.json so that the agent respects the per-session model pins sent via session/set_config_option.

<type>discovery</type>
<title>Root cause found: Claude Code settings.json model override</title>
<subtitle>Global model setting in Claude agent config overrides bridge's per-session model pins</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>~/.claude/settings.json contains "model": "opus[1m]" as a global agent-level setting</fact>
<fact>Bridge correctly configures "model": "claude-fable-5" in acp-providers.json for "claude" backend</fact>
<fact>Bridge passes backend.model to AcpProvider which calls session/set_config_option at session startup</fact>
<fact>Claude Code agent's global settings.json model takes precedence over per-session set_config_option calls</fact>
<fact>Configuration hierarchy: agent settings.json (global) > session/set_config_option (per-session) > .env (default)</fact>
<fact>User previously set opus[1m] in agent settings, likely during /model testing or experimentation</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>The root cause has been identified through settings file inspection. The user's ~/.claude/settings.json contains a global "model": "opus[1m]" configuration that applies to all sessions started by the Claude Code agent, regardless of what the bridge attempts to configure via the ACP session/set_config_option mechanism. This explains the entire mystery: the bridge's configuration is correct (acp-providers.json specifies fable-5, the code properly passes it to AcpProvider, and applyModelEffort() correctly calls set_config_option), but the Claude agent itself ignores these per-session pins because its own global configuration takes precedence. The configuration hierarchy works as: agent-level settings.json (highest precedence) > per-session set_config_option (middle) > .env defaults (lowest). The user likely set this opus[1m] model in their Claude settings previously during testing or when using /model command in an interactive session, and forgot it was persisted globally. The fix is to remove the "model" line from settings.json so that the agent respects the bridge's per-session model configuration sent via set_config_option.</narrative>
